name: auth
title: Auth (Supabase email/password)
description: Supabase email/password auth: /login page, /auth/callback route, proxy.ts session refresh + protected-route gating, and the lib/supabase client/server/admin/proxy helpers. Ships with supabase/seed.sql demo users (demo1@example.com, demo2@example.com, demo3@example.com — password: demo-password-123). Requires NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (server-only, used by lib/supabase/admin.ts).

files.content:
-- Demo users for local development only. Password for every account below
-- is the same: demo-password-123
--
--   demo1@example.com
--   demo2@example.com
--   demo3@example.com
--
-- Identity lives entirely on auth.users / auth.identities (Supabase Auth) --
-- there is no parallel public.users table in this template. Sign in at
-- /login with any address above and the shared password; email confirmation
-- is disabled locally (see supabase/config.toml) so login works immediately.
--
-- This relies on direct INSERTs into auth.users, which requires the
-- Postgres superuser role that `supabase start` grants locally. Never run
-- this against a hosted/deployed Supabase project -- create real users with
-- supabase.auth.admin.createUser() (see src/lib/supabase/admin.ts) there
-- instead. Runs automatically on `supabase db reset` (see [db.seed] in
-- supabase/config.toml) and is safe to re-run: fixed UUIDs + ON CONFLICT DO
-- NOTHING skip rows that already exist.

create extension if not exists "pgcrypto";

insert into auth.users (
  instance_id,
  id,
  aud,
  role,
  email,
  encrypted_password,
  email_confirmed_at,
  raw_app_meta_data,
  raw_user_meta_data,
  created_at,
  updated_at,
  confirmation_token,
  email_change,
  email_change_token_new,
  recovery_token
) values
  (
    '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000',
    '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111',
    'authenticated',
    'authenticated',
    'demo1@example.com',
    crypt('demo-password-123', gen_salt('bf')),
    now(),
    '{"provider":"email","providers":["email"]}'::jsonb,
    '{"full_name":"Demo One"}'::jsonb,
    now(),
    now(),
    '', '', '', ''
  ),
  (
    '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000',
    '22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222',
    'authenticated',
    'authenticated',
    'demo2@example.com',
    crypt('demo-password-123', gen_salt('bf')),
    now(),
    '{"provider":"email","providers":["email"]}'::jsonb,
    '{"full_name":"Demo Two"}'::jsonb,
    now(),
    now(),
    '', '', '', ''
  ),
  (
    '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000',
    '33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333333333',
    'authenticated',
    'authenticated',
    'demo3@example.com',
    crypt('demo-password-123', gen_salt('bf')),
    now(),
    '{"provider":"email","providers":["email"]}'::jsonb,
    '{"full_name":"Demo Three"}'::jsonb,
    now(),
    now(),
    '', '', '', ''
  )
on conflict (id) do nothing;

-- auth.identities needs a companion row per user for email/password sign-in
-- to work (Supabase Auth resolves the identity, not just the users row).
insert into auth.identities (
  id,
  user_id,
  provider_id,
  identity_data,
  provider,
  last_sign_in_at,
  created_at,
  updated_at
)
select
  u.id,
  u.id,
  u.id::text,
  jsonb_build_object('sub', u.id::text, 'email', u.email),
  'email',
  now(),
  now(),
  now()
from auth.users u
where u.email in ('demo1@example.com', 'demo2@example.com', 'demo3@example.com')
on conflict (id) do nothing;
